questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All pigs are elephants. Some bakers are not pigs. Conclusions: I. Some bakers are not pigs. II. Some pigs are not bakers. III. Some elephants are not bakers. IV. Some bakers are not elephants.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All ribs are tips. Many ribs are ribs. Conclusions: I. Some ribs are tips. II. Some tips are ribs. III. All ribs are ribs. IV. Some tips are not ribs.

choices.e: I and II and either III or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some charts are darts. All darts are carts. Some carts are smarts. Conclusions: I. Some charts are carts. II. Some carts are darts. III. Some darts are smarts. IV. Some smarts are charts.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All boxes are tables. No desk is table. Some desks are curtains. Conclusions: I. No boxes are desks. II. Some boxes are not desks. III. Some curtains are not boxes. IV. Some curtains are boxes.

choices.a: III and either I or II follow
choices.b: I and either III or IV follow
choices.c: Either I or II and either III or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some drops are cops. All cops are docks. No dock is a flop. Conclusions: I. Some drops are not flops. II. Some dockings are drops. III. No cop is a flop. IV. Some flops are not cops.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some big are small. No small is large. Some large are tiny. Conclusions: I. Some large are not big. II. No big is large. III. Some small are not tiny. IV. Some big are not tiny.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No student is decent. Some decent are bags. All bags are roses. Conclusions: I. Some bags are not students. II. All bags are students. III. Some decents are roses. IV. All roses are decent.

choices.e: Either I or II, and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some birds are stones. Some tigers are birds. All stones are grapes. Conclusions: I. Some stones are birds. II. Some stones are not birds. III. Some grapes are birds. IV. Some tigers are stones.

choices.c: Either I or II and III follow
